km_name: SpecificTopic Binary KM - Biodiversity, Ecosystems, Land and Animal Welfare
purpose: Binary Yes/No SpecificTopic decisions for the biodiversity_ecosystems_land family. This simplified KM owns its tag-level reconciliation and emits only final Yes tags for this family.

rules: Answer every tag in this KM independently as Yes or No before producing the family final list.
Emit only tags answered Yes in final_specific_topics_from_this_family.
Do not emit candidates, centrality labels, or nearby labels as final tags.
If two tags overlap, keep both only when the source has distinct operative evidence for both; otherwise answer Yes only for the more directly satisfied decision question.
Reject incidental, background, implementation-actor-only, and keyword-only evidence even when a semantic signal appears.
The standalone SpecificTopic reconciler has been removed; this family KM is responsible for suppressing weak/adjacent labels before code consolidation.
weak_evidence_policy: Weak or not-observed tags remain in the enum, but need direct source evidence at runtime.
Do not use weak master examples as independent proof; use them only as cautionary boundary tests.
weak_or_not_observed_tags_in_this_family: Direct Impact Drivers of Biodiversity Loss
Impacts on the State of Species
Impacts on the Extent and Conditions of Ecosystems
Impacts on and Dependencies on Ecosystem Services

tag_decisions.tag: canonical SpecificTopic value from this KM
tag_decisions.evidence_excerpt: required when answer is Yes; empty string allowed when No
tag_decisions.nearby_label_if_any: optional canonical tag that better fits the evidence

final_specific_topics_from_this_family: all tags from tag_decisions with answer=Yes, already reconciled by this family KM

tags.definition: Spatial planning, zoning, environmental assessment of plans/projects, development control, or land-use decision-making.
tags.decision_question: Should this source be tagged SpecificTopic = "Land Use Planning"?
yes_when: The instrument centrally regulates planning approvals, land-use plans, EIA/SEA, zoning, project consent, or spatial development impacts.
There is a source-supported evidence role: regulated_object, legal_mechanism, protected_interest, direct_addressee, direct_regulated_product/facility, or final-topic ESRS mapping trace.
The tag is primary, companion, or benchmark_expected_companion; it is not merely adjacent or incidental.
no_when: The source merely affects land indirectly through environmental rules, biodiversity protection, or soil clean-up without a planning mechanism.
The only support is a keyword hit, source URL/domain/slug, filename, row ID, benchmark answer, generic legal boilerplate, or implementation/enforcement actor.
A narrower or different canonical label captures the operative source evidence more accurately.

test_case.question: A source's title/scope/operative provisions centrally say: "The location of a landfill must take into consideration requirements relating to: (a) the distances from the boundary of the site to residential and recreation areas, waterways, water bodies and other agricultural or urban sites;". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Land Use Planning"? Answer Yes/No and cite the evidence role.
test_case.reasoning_check: Yes if the quoted evidence satisfies the tag_when rule: The instrument centrally regulates planning approvals, land-use plans, EIA/SEA, zoning, project consent, or spatial development impacts.
forces_reasoning_about: whether the excerpt is source evidence rather than a benchmark artifact

test_case.question: A source mentions "land use" once in background, but its operative duties regulate a different subject and no duty matches "The instrument centrally regulates planning approvals, land-use plans, EIA/SEA, zoning, project consent, or spatial development impacts.". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Land Use Planning"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. A keyword or background reference is not enough; the label needs source-supported central evidence.

test_case.question: A source fits this exclusion: "The source merely affects land indirectly through environmental rules, biodiversity protection, or soil clean-up without a planning mechanism.". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Land Use Planning"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. The label-specific do_not_tag_when rule excludes this case.

test_case.question: The master evidence includes a weak/indirect edge example: "Declaration may be subject to condition about obtaining regulatory approvals". Standing alone, should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Land Use Planning"?
test_case.reasoning_check: The master reason was classified as weak/cautionary; the LLM must look for central legal duties, not rely on weak contextual association.

tags.definition: Protection, conservation, sustainable use, monitoring, or governance of biological diversity, habitats, species, or ecosystems.
tags.decision_question: Should this source be tagged SpecificTopic = "Biodiversity Conservation"?
yes_when: The source centrally protects biodiversity, species, habitats, ecosystems, conservation status, Natura 2000, nature protection, or biodiversity impacts.
There is a source-supported evidence role: regulated_object, legal_mechanism, protected_interest, direct_addressee, direct_regulated_product/facility, or final-topic ESRS mapping trace.
The tag is primary, companion, or benchmark_expected_companion; it is not merely adjacent or incidental.
no_when: Do not tag merely because an environmental assessment or planning rule mentions flora, fauna, or environment in passing. Require biodiversity, species, habitats, ecosystems, or nature protection as a central protected interest.
The only support is a keyword hit, source URL/domain/slug, filename, row ID, benchmark answer, generic legal boilerplate, or implementation/enforcement actor.
A narrower or different canonical label captures the operative source evidence more accurately.

test_case.question: A source mentions "biodiversity" once in background, but its operative duties regulate a different subject and no duty matches "The source centrally protects biodiversity, species, habitats, ecosystems, conservation status, Natura 2000, nature protection, or biodiversity impacts.". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Biodiversity Conservation"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. A keyword or background reference is not enough; the label needs source-supported central evidence.

test_case.question: A source fits this exclusion: "Do not tag merely because an environmental assessment or planning rule mentions flora, fauna, or environment in passing. tags.tag: Direct Impact Drivers of Biodiversity Loss
tags.definition: Drivers that directly cause biodiversity loss, including land-use change, exploitation, invasive species, pollution, climate change, and ecosystem disturbance.
tags.decision_question: Should this source be tagged SpecificTopic = "Direct Impact Drivers of Biodiversity Loss"?
yes_when: The source identifies or regulates direct biodiversity-loss drivers as a substantive topic, not merely a generic environmental effect.
There is a source-supported evidence role: regulated_object, legal_mechanism, protected_interest, direct_addressee, direct_regulated_product/facility, or final-topic ESRS mapping trace.
The tag is primary, companion, or benchmark_expected_companion; it is not merely adjacent or incidental.

Evidence-packet support for this tag is weak, indirect, or absent; answer Yes only when the current source text independently and directly satisfies the definition, decision question, and a yes_when rule.
no_when: The law targets a single driver such as pollution or climate without linking it to biodiversity impacts.
The only support is a keyword hit, source URL/domain/slug, filename, row ID, benchmark answer, generic legal boilerplate, or implementation/enforcement actor.
A narrower or different canonical label captures the operative source evidence more accurately.
Do not answer Yes from analogy to weak master examples, broad ESG relatedness, a title-only hunch, or the absence of a better label.

tags.tag: Impacts on and Dependencies on Ecosystem Services
tags.definition: Impacts on, risks to, or dependencies on services that ecosystems provide to people and businesses.
tags.decision_question: Should this source be tagged SpecificTopic = "Impacts on and Dependencies on Ecosystem Services"?
yes_when: The source directly references ecosystem services, dependencies, nature-related services, or impacts on the benefits provided by ecosystems.
There is a source-supported evidence role: regulated_object, legal_mechanism, protected_interest, direct_addressee, direct_regulated_product/facility, or final-topic ESRS mapping trace.
The tag is primary, companion, or benchmark_expected_companion; it is not merely adjacent or incidental.

Evidence-packet support for this tag is weak, indirect, or absent; answer Yes only when the current source text independently and directly satisfies the definition, decision question, and a yes_when rule.
no_when: The source protects biodiversity generally without service/dependency concepts.
The only support is a keyword hit, source URL/domain/slug, filename, row ID, benchmark answer, generic legal boilerplate, or implementation/enforcement actor.
A narrower or different canonical label captures the operative source evidence more accurately.
Do not answer Yes from analogy to weak master examples, broad ESG relatedness, a title-only hunch, or the absence of a better label.

test_case.id: SpecificTopic.Impacts on and Dependencies on Ecosystem Services.positive_direct
test_case.question: A source's title/scope/operative provisions centrally say: "The source directly references ecosystem services, dependencies, nature-related services, or impacts on the benefits provided by ecosystems.". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Impacts on and Dependencies on Ecosystem Services"? Answer Yes/No and cite the evidence role.
test_case.reasoning_check: Yes if the quoted evidence satisfies the tag_when rule: The source directly references ecosystem services, dependencies, nature-related services, or impacts on the benefits provided by ecosystems.
forces_reasoning_about: whether the excerpt is source evidence rather than a benchmark artifact

test_case.id: SpecificTopic.Impacts on and Dependencies on Ecosystem Services.negative_incidental_keyword
test_case.question: A source mentions "ecosystem services" once in background, but its operative duties regulate a different subject and no duty matches "The source directly references ecosystem services, dependencies, nature-related services, or impacts on the benefits provided by ecosystems.". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Impacts on and Dependencies on Ecosystem Services"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. A keyword or background reference is not enough; the label needs source-supported central evidence.

test_case.id: SpecificTopic.Impacts on and Dependencies on Ecosystem Services.boundary_exclusion
test_case.question: A source fits this exclusion: "The source protects biodiversity generally without service/dependency concepts.". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Impacts on and Dependencies on Ecosystem Services"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. The label-specific do_not_tag_when rule excludes this case.

tags.definition: Protection of soil as a natural resource from erosion, degradation, sealing, compaction, or loss of productive capacity.
tags.decision_question: Should this source be tagged SpecificTopic = "Soil Conservation"?
yes_when: The source centrally addresses soil protection, conservation, land degradation, erosion control, or sustainable soil management.
There is a source-supported evidence role: regulated_object, legal_mechanism, protected_interest, direct_addressee, direct_regulated_product/facility, or final-topic ESRS mapping trace.
The tag is primary, companion, or benchmark_expected_companion; it is not merely adjacent or incidental.
no_when: The main concern is contaminated land clean-up or pollutant thresholds rather than soil conservation.
The only support is a keyword hit, source URL/domain/slug, filename, row ID, benchmark answer, generic legal boilerplate, or implementation/enforcement actor.
A narrower or different canonical label captures the operative source evidence more accurately.

tags.definition: Protection of animal health, humane treatment, husbandry, transport, slaughter, testing, or welfare conditions.

tags.definition: Designation, management, restrictions, or conservation objectives for protected areas in marine or coastal waters.
tags.decision_question: Should this source be tagged SpecificTopic = "Marine Protected Areas"?
yes_when: The source creates or regulates marine protected areas, protected marine zones, special conservation areas at sea, or restrictions inside those areas.
There is a source-supported evidence role: regulated_object, legal_mechanism, protected_interest, direct_addressee, direct_regulated_product/facility, or final-topic ESRS mapping trace.
The tag is primary, companion, or benchmark_expected_companion; it is not merely adjacent or incidental.
no_when: The law addresses marine pollution, fisheries, or marine strategy without protected-area designation or management.
The only support is a keyword hit, source URL/domain/slug, filename, row ID, benchmark answer, generic legal boilerplate, or implementation/enforcement actor.
A narrower or different canonical label captures the operative source evidence more accurately.

Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Marine Protected Areas"? Answer Yes/No and cite the evidence role.
test_case.reasoning_check: Yes if the quoted evidence satisfies the tag_when rule: The source creates or regulates marine protected areas, protected marine zones, special conservation areas at sea, or restrictions inside those areas.
